title: VS Code surface (v0.17): Memory panel + Journey + Why/Capture — the README/marketplace contract
context: The extension grew from the Journey-embed era into the Hunch Memory panel era (timeline, inline escalations, constitution authority lifecycle, self-repair moves). The marketplace README was rewritten for v1.8/v0.17 and the topic chain needed its current link so the doc anchors to live intent.
decision: The extension's public contract is three surfaces: (1) the Hunch Memory activity-bar panel — memory-move timeline with click-to-diff and local revert, ⚖ Needs-your-decision escalations, 🏛 Constitution group with one-click audited activate/demote/withdraw/retire, strictness switch, approve-to-push; (2) Journey — the read-only compounding view with the embedded memory graph; (3) Why/Capture — the per-file read and the delegated write. All writes delegate to the hunch CLI/MCP; the panel spawn seam is conformance-certified against CLI/MCP/CI receipts.
consequences: vscode-extension/README.md reconciles to this decision — the anchor-stale drift closes.
Future surface changes supersede THIS record on the same topic.
alternatives_rejected: Leaving the topic chain ending at the Journey-era decision (rejected: the README describes the panel era; a stale anchor makes grounding lie)
Renaming the topic to vscode-extension.scope to merge families (rejected: renames break the existing anchor history; one chain per family, superseded links preserved)
